---
title: "UIAO Academy"
subtitle: "Learner-facing entry point — navigate the canon by track, level, or comic"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "One page to browse UIAO by track (Contributor, Operator), by the education comic arc, or by a reading level (Orientation → Capstone). All modules reference canonical UIAO_NNN specs; the Academy is a rendered consumer view, not a parallel curriculum."
aspirational: true
---

The UIAO Academy is the **learner-facing entry point** to the canon.
It does not invent curriculum — it presents the canon in three
reader-friendly paths: by **track** (contributor vs. operator),
by **comic** (education arc), and by **level** (orientation to
capstone). Every module below references a canonical UIAO_NNN
document; the Academy is a rendered consumer view, governed by
the canon it cites.

## Canon anchors (the substrate this Academy consumes)

| Anchor | Purpose in the Academy |
|---|---|
| [UIAO_125 Training Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-training.md) | Defines the **two tracks** (Contributor + Operator) and the shared core every learner takes |
| [UIAO_126 Test Plans Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-test-plans.md) | Catalogues the **test-plan artifacts** learners will produce or consume |
| [UIAO_127 Project Plans Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-project-plans.md) | Provides the **agency templates** used in the capstone |
| [UIAO_128 Education Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-education.md) | Defines the **comic-led learning arc** for external (agency) onboarding |
| [UIAO_131 Adapter Test Strategy](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-test-strategy.md) | Tier-1/2/3 test framework; learners on the Contributor track learn to produce evidence against it |

---

## Pick your path

### If you're new to UIAO — **start with the comics**

The Education Program (UIAO_128) uses eight illustrated comics as
the top of the funnel. Each comic is one abstraction, one image,
one short walkthrough. You don't need to know anything yet.

| Comic | What it shows | Companion reading |
|---|---|---|
| The Governance OS | What UIAO *is* — an operating system for governance | [Narrative walkthrough](../narrative/program-education.qmd) |
| The Canonical Object | How one object gets one canonical record | [UIAO_001 SSOT](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO-SSOT.md) |
| The Unified Environment Map | How adapters collapse many vendors into one map | [UIAO_003 Adapter Segmentation](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_003_Adapter_Segmentation_Overview_v1.0.md) |
| Provenance | Why every artifact traces back to a canonical source | [UIAO_113 Evidence Graph](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/graph-schema.md) |
| The Modernization Lifecycle | How a modernization runs end-to-end | [UIAO_124 Adapter Ops Runbook](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-operations-runbook.md) |
| Address Governance | The directory / IPAM flow | [Governance-OS migration narrative](../narrative/governance-os-directory-migration.md) |
| Drift Detection | The closed loop: scan → classify → remediate | [UIAO_110 Drift Engine](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/drift.md) |
| Drift Remediation | How a finding becomes a closed ticket | [UIAO_117 Recovery Layer](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/recovery.md) |

After the comics, pick a track below.

### If you're going to contribute — **Contributor track**

UIAO_125 §1.2. You come out of this able to land a canon-compliant
PR without review blockers.

- **Foundation** — Read UIAO_001 (SSOT), UIAO_003 (Adapter Segmentation), UIAO_200 (Substrate Manifest), UIAO_201 (Workspace Contract). Run `uiao substrate walk` on a clone.
- **Canon change protocol** — Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [ADR-028](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-028-monorepo-consolidation-gos-integration.md). Write and land one ADR.
- **Schema authoring** — Read [`src/uiao/schemas/`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/core/schemas). Write a JSON Schema that validates cleanly against an existing registry.
- **Adapter development** — Read UIAO_121 (Conformance Test Plan), UIAO_123 (Integration Test Plan), UIAO_124 (Ops Runbook), UIAO_131 (Test Strategy). Build an adapter against tier-1 (commercial tenant) + tier-2 (fixture-based) evidence.
- **Release engineering** — Read UIAO_118 (Release Engineering). Cut a release with sigstore signing and CycloneDX SBOM.

### If you're adopting UIAO in an agency — **Operator track**

UIAO_125 §1.3. You come out of this able to run the substrate
against your own environment and interpret its outputs.

- **Substrate walker** — Run `uiao substrate walk` first on any fresh clone. Read [Substrate Status](../docs/substrate-status.qmd) to see the honest reality map.
- **Drift interpretation** — Read the [Drift Detection Standard](../docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd). Map a P1/P2 finding to a remediation path.
- **Evidence pipeline** — Read UIAO_107 (Collector Interface), UIAO_113 (Evidence Graph). Run an adapter and watch its OSCAL bundle land in `impl/out/`.
- **Canon-safe customization** — Read UIAO_127 (Project Plans) for the four agency templates. Instantiate one.
- **KSI attestation** — Generate and verify an evidence bundle.

---

## Levels (alternative path — if you prefer reading by depth)

The Academy is also browsable by level. This is useful when you
know the depth you want but not the role.

### Level 0 — Orientation

Who UIAO is for. Why it exists. 30-minute read.

- [Why modernization is hard](../narrative/why-modernization-is-hard.qmd) (narrative)
- [Governance-OS directory migration](../narrative/governance-os-directory-migration.md) (narrative)
- [UIAO Narrative Layer](../narrative/UIAO-Narrative-Layer.md) (the seven narrative constructs)

### Level 1 — Foundations

What UIAO actually is. 90-minute read.

- [UIAO_001 SSOT](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO-SSOT.md)
- [UIAO_003 Adapter Segmentation](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_003_Adapter_Segmentation_Overview_v1.0.md)
- [UIAO_101 Platform Overview](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/Platform-Overview.md)
- [UIAO_200 Substrate Manifest](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/substrate-manifest.yaml)
- [UIAO_201 Workspace Contract](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/workspace-contract.yaml)

### Level 2 — Governance & Compliance

How UIAO handles compliance as a product, not a project.

- [UIAO_002 SCuBA Technical Spec](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_002_SCuBA_Technical_Specification_v1.0.md)
- [UIAO_004 Executive Orders](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/compliance/executive-orders.md)
- [UIAO_112 Multi-Tenant Isolation](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/governance.md)
- [UIAO_120 Zero-Trust Integration](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/zero-trust.md)
- [FedRAMP 20x Crosswalk](../docs/03_FedRAMP20x_Crosswalk.qmd)

### Level 3 — Data & Runtime Controls

Evidence, drift, consent, claims. The runtime mechanics.

- [UIAO_107 Collector Interface](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/collector-interface.md)
- [UIAO_109 Data Lake Model](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/data-lake.md)
- [UIAO_110 Drift Engine](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/drift.md)
- [UIAO_113 Evidence Graph](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/graph-schema.md)
- [UIAO_129 Application Identity Model](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/application-identity-model.md)

### Level 4 — Applied UIAO (capstone)

Produce a UIAO-aligned modernization plan for a real or hypothetical system.

- Read [UIAO_127 Project Plans Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-project-plans.md) and pick one of the four agency templates (greenfield / directory-migration / SCuBA conformance / OSCAL ATO).
- Instantiate the template. Produce: current state, target UIAO-aligned state, control-plane mapping, governance objects, phased rollout.
- Run `uiao substrate walk` on your plan. Address every P1 / P2 finding.

---

## Not in this page (by design)

- **New curriculum doctrine.** UIAO_125 / UIAO_128 already define the curriculum; this page is the reader-facing shell that surfaces them.
- **Sequencing enforcement.** You can enter at any path. The Academy presents choices; it does not gate.
- **Certification.** UIAO_125 §4 defines completion criteria (land a PR for Contributor track; clean walker/drift run for Operator track). No parallel certification system.

## Why this page is aspirational

UIAO_125 and UIAO_128 are declared canon but not yet in routine
operational use (no running Training cohorts, no facilitated
Education sessions yet). The Academy is the rendered consumer view
of programs that will become aspirational-free as they're adopted
and exercised. Tracking state lives on the
[Substrate Status](../docs/substrate-status.qmd) page.

---

## Next

Each path below goes to a dedicated page with stage-by-stage walkthrough:

- **New to UIAO?** → [Comic tour](comics.qmd) (~45 minutes, eight abstractions)
- **Contributing?** → [Contributor track](contributor-track.qmd) (Stage 1 Foundation → Stage 5 Release engineering)
- **Adopting in an agency?** → [Operator track](operator-track.qmd) (Stage 1 Substrate walker → Stage 5 KSI attestation)
- **Just browsing?** Pick a **Level** from the section above.
